title: Add lock renumbering
body:
Still needs Podman frontend, plus a bit more lock code.

Needed for a 1.1 release. We also need to modify RPM postinstall to call this for any 1.0->1.1 upgrades to ensure all containers get migrated properly to SHM locks.

Still messy but feel free to review while I hack the last bits into place.

comments.body: (Hopefully the main body of the code is pretty clean - this is a rewrite of an earlier effort that turned into a sprawling mess)

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Alright, code should all be here. Still need to do some testing (and see if I can add tests without a massive hassle), but I'll remove the WIP tag, this is ready for review.
Squashed some of the tangential commits together so build_each_commit won't time out

Works in all my local testing. It even seems to be same to run while other Podman instances are going, which I wasn't expecting. I really wouldn't recommend doing that, but if you do nothing seems to explode.

@rhatdan @baude @TomSweeneyRedHat @vrothberg PTAL
(Still needs manpages + completions. I don't think we can test this in CI right now - it requires a 1.0 or lower Podman to actually do anything)
To clarify, you need both a 1.0 or lower Podman, and the latest master with renumber patches, to actually test
Why change 100 interfaces to all call false, and only one to call true?  Wouldn't it have been easier to add one new interface?
This is what happens when I code before coffee. Fixed.

@vrothberg The locks were only used in one place, RemoveVolume (and not even everywhere there - there were cases where we'd remove without locks), so rather than try and add them to renumber I just removed them.. They didn't seem to be doing anything
Thanks for adding the comments, @mheon. Will a manpage be added with a later commit?
I was meaning to do that yesterday, but it completely slipped my mind and I just hit review comments. Oops. Manpage incoming.

Manpages finally added, managed to get distracted again.

@rhatdan @vrothberg @baude PTAL

Squashed more so build_each_commit doesn't time out.

@rhatdan @baude @vrothberg @TomSweeneyRedHat @giuseppe PTAL, let's get this merged for 1.1.0

@mheon, sorry for not noticing this earlier. libpod.conf doesn't have an option for this now right? It's manpage is also missing an entry.

Besides that LGTM.
